description: All TPM operations (NV read, NV write, sign, read_public) use execute_with_nullauth_session (secretx-tpm2/src/lib.rs:220,270,318,324). NV operations use NvAuth::Owner, meaning the NV index must be defined with owner hierarchy authorization = empty password. Signing operations similarly assume no key authorization policy. Neither the module docs nor the from_uri docs mention this requirement. Callers provisioning NV indices or keys with non-empty auth passwords (or HMAC/policy sessions) will get opaque TPM errors. Document the auth model assumption. If auth-password support is planned, consider a ?auth= URI parameter (analogous to PKCS#11's ?pin= parameter).
title: tpm2: nullauth-only session model is undocumented
